name: Gollizno Route Trail
description: Tackle the moderate 11.2-mile El Castillejo circular mountain bike route from Moclín, featuring 2914 feet of climbing and views of Moclín Ca
region_intro: Mountain biking around Moclín offers a diverse landscape characterized by rolling hills, natural reservoirs, and varied terrain suitable for off-road cycling. The region features a mix of open paths and more technical sections, providing different challenges for mountain bikers. Prominent features include the Sierra Elvira mountain range and the Colomera and Cubillas reservoirs, which shape many of the local routes. This area provides a natural setting for mountain biking, with trails winding through the Spanish countryside. Best mountain bike trails around Moclín The most popular mountain bike trail is Sierra Elvira Loop, a 18.2 miles (29.2 km) trail that takes 2 hours 36 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers panoramic views of the surrounding hills. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Colomera Reservoir Loop, a moderate 20.1 miles (32.4 km) path. This route navigates along the reservoir's edge, featuring a mix of open and wooded sections. Local mountain bikers also love the Gollizno Route Trail, a 11.2 miles (18.0 km) trail leading through varied natural landscapes, often completed in about 2 hours 11 minutes. Mountain biking around Moclín is defined by hilly terrain, reservoir paths, and natural landscapes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate loops to more challenging trails. The routes in Moclín are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 60 reviews. More than 850 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Moclín's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Moclín?
A: There are over 30 mountain bike trails around Moclín, offering a variety of experiences for different skill levels. The region's landscape features rolling hills, natural reservoirs, and varied terrain.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Moclín?
A: Yes, Moclín offers several options for beginners. There are 4 easy mountain bike trails, perfect for those new to the sport or looking for a more relaxed ride. These routes typically feature less challenging terrain and shorter distances.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Moclín?
A: Mountain biking around Moclín is characterized by a diverse landscape including rolling hills, paths along natural reservoirs like Colomera and Cubillas, and varied terrain. You'll find a mix of open paths and some more technical sections, often winding through the Spanish countryside.

Q: Are there any challenging mountain bike trails for experienced riders?
A: Yes, Moclín has 14 difficult mountain bike trails for experienced riders seeking a challenge. These routes often feature significant elevation changes and more technical sections, providing a demanding ride through the region's hilly terrain.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails that are loop routes?
A: Many of the mountain bike trails around Moclín are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1017238444/sierra-elvira-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra Elvira Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1017567920/colomera-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Colomera Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which navigate along the reservoir's edge.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or attractions I can see near the mountain bike trails?
A: While mountain biking, you can explore several historical and natural landmarks. Nearby attractions include the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5277382" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Moclín Castle&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5411678" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of Saint Anthony&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3682350" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pinos Puente Bridge&lt;/a&gt;. The routes also often pass by the scenic Colomera and Cubillas reservoirs.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about the trails in Moclín?
A: The mountain bike trails in Moclín are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 60 reviews. Riders often praise the diverse landscape, the mix of open and wooded sections, and the panoramic views of the surrounding hills, particularly on routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1030998844/gollizno-route-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gollizno Route Trail&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best season for mountain biking in Moclín?
A: The best seasons for mountain biking in Moclín are typically spring and autumn. During these times, the weather is generally mild and pleasant, making for comfortable riding conditions. Summers can be very hot, and winters can bring cooler temperatures, though riding is often still possible.

Q: Are there any mountain bike routes that explore the Cubillas Reservoir?
A: Yes, the Cubillas Reservoir is a prominent feature in the region and several mountain bike routes explore its surroundings. You can find routes such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40376831" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Shore of the Cubillas Reservoir loop&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32040623" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cubillas Reservoir loop from Olivares&lt;/a&gt;, offering scenic rides along its edges.

Q: What are the typical distances and durations for mountain bike trails in Moclín?
A: Trail distances vary, with moderate routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1017238444/sierra-elvira-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra Elvira Loop&lt;/a&gt; covering about 18.2 miles (29.2 km) and taking around 2 hours 36 minutes. Longer routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1017567920/colomera-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Colomera Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt;, are about 20.1 miles (32.4 km) and can take closer to 3 hours 13 minutes.

Q: Is parking generally available near mountain bike trailheads in Moclín?
A: While specific parking information for each trailhead varies, Moclín and its surrounding villages typically offer public parking areas. It's advisable to check the starting point of your chosen route on komoot for more precise parking details or local recommendations.
tips:
Located in Moclín, Granada, crowning a hill at approximately 1100 meters above sea level, the fortress stands atop a natural rocky outcrop, overlooking the town and the Granada plain.

Also known as Hisn al-Muqlin (“Fortress of the Two Pupils”) during the Nasrid period.

Built by the Nasrids in the mid-14th century, after the fall of fortresses such as Alcalá la Real, to reinforce the border with Castile; situated within the northwestern defensive system of the Kingdom of Granada.
It suffered severe damage during the Spanish Civil War, was used as a cemetery, and was bombed; partial restorations began in 2010 and continued between 2012 and 2013, although signs of neglect are still evident.

Visiting Moclín is usually a highly recommended experience, especially if you enjoy destinations that combine history, nature, and tranquility.

The best of Moclín:
Impressive historical heritage: The Nasrid castle and church are true gems for lovers of medieval history.
Spectacular natural surroundings: The Gollizno Route is ideal for hiking, with suspension bridges and viewpoints overlooking the valley.
Charming villages: Cobblestone streets, whitewashed houses, and photogenic corners.
Authentic experience: It's not a crowded destination, allowing you to enjoy it at a leisurely pace.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the El Castillejo — circular desde Moclín route?
faqs.answer: This mountain bike route is designed to be completed in approximately 2 hours and 11 minutes. However, your actual time may vary depending on your fitness level, riding speed, and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this route is rated as 'moderate' for mountain biking. It requires good fitness and advanced riding skills, roughly corresponding to an S2 rating on the Single Track Scale. While not exclusively for experts, beginners may find some sections technically challenging due to narrow paths, strong ascents, and rocky areas.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the El Castillejo mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: The terrain features a mix of narrow paths, significant ascents, and descents, with some rocky sections. You'll ride through diverse landscapes, including extensive olive groves and river valleys. Be prepared for varied surfaces that demand good bike handling skills.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest or views along the route?
faqs.answer: Along the route, you'll encounter historical and natural beauty. Key highlights include the historic Moclín Castle and panoramic views of Moclín and its castle. You can also expect impressive vistas of the Vega de Granada and the distant Sierra Nevada mountains, all set amidst a 'sea of olive trees' and river valleys.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this route?
faqs.answer: The best times to ride this route are typically spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November). During these seasons, the weather is generally milder and more pleasant for mountain biking, allowing you to avoid the intense heat of summer and potential colder, wetter conditions in winter.

faqs.question: Where can I park and access the trailhead for the El Castillejo route?
faqs.answer: The route starts and ends in Moclín. While specific parking details aren't provided, you should look for public parking areas within the village of Moclín itself. The trailhead is easily accessible from the village center.

faqs.question: Is the El Castillejo — circular desde Moclín route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While dogs are generally welcome on many trails in the region, it's always recommended to keep them on a leash, especially given the moderate difficulty, potential for technical sections, and the presence of wildlife or other trail users. Please ensure your dog is fit enough for the distance and elevation changes.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to ride this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to access and ride the El Castillejo — circular desde Moclín mountain bike route. It is generally open to the public.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route shares sections with several other trails. Notably, it covers 51% of the Ruta del Gollizno and 25% of the Camino Mozárabe. Smaller portions also overlap with the Camino de Corcuela, Camino de Tózar a Colomera, and Vereda de Majadillas.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or pubs near the trail?
faqs.answer: As the route starts and ends in Moclín, you will find local cafes and pubs within the village where you can refuel before or after your ride. It's advisable to check their operating hours, especially if you plan to ride early or late.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a mountain bike ride on this trail?
faqs.answer: Given the moderate difficulty and varied terrain, you should bring plenty of water, energy snacks, a basic bike repair kit (including a spare tube), a first-aid kit, and appropriate mountain biking gear. Wearing a helmet is essential. Consider bringing a map or GPS device, as cell service can be unreliable in some areas.

waypoints.name: View of Moclín and Moclín Castle
